statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find the minimum value in the array. If all elements are equal, return any of the elements. If the array is empty, return -Infinity.
statement.outputFormat: The minimum value in the array, e.g., 1
statement.notes: Do not modify the original array.

examples.explanation: The minimum value in the array is 1.
All elements are equal, so return any of them.
The array is empty, so return -Infinity.

hints.text: Use the Math.min() function to find the minimum value.

editorial.title: Array Min Finder Solution
editorial.intuition: The problem requires finding the minimum value in an array. We can use the Math.min() function to achieve this.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a loop to iterate through the array and keep track of the minimum value.
editorial.betterSolution: Using the Math.min() function is more efficient than using a loop.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the Math.min() function with the spread operator.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the Math.min() function returns the smallest value in the array.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case where the array is empty.

starterCode.javascript: var findMin = function(nums) { // TO DO: Implement the logic to find the minimum value in the array };

buggyStarterCode.javascript: var findMin = function(nums) { if (nums.length === 0) return -Infinity; var min = nums[0]; for (var i = 1; i < nums.length; i++) { if (nums[i] < min) min = nums[i]; } return min; }
